figures.caption: Figure 1: From left to right: a) a slab of aluminium punctured upon hypervelocity impact; b) the Whipple shield consists of a bumper made of thin aluminium sheet in front of a spacecraft hull; c) imaging of debris cloud formed when a projectile hit the “meteor bumper”; and d) a simulation of hypervelocity impact with a resolution of approximately 1M particles
